title: Independent verification of final JSON report
scope: Audited the final JSON report for parseability, source support, internal consistency, completeness against the requested VS Code plus Obsidian autocomplete question, and current high-risk factual claims. Used narrow ketch checks and no browser automation.
summary: The final report answers the requested question for both VS Code and Obsidian and its core conclusion is supported: official OpenAI Codex and Claude Code can use eligible subscriptions for agent/chat/edit workflows, but current checked official docs do not document Copilot-style live inline Tab autocomplete; practical true autocomplete paths use local models, product-specific autocomplete services, or separately billed APIs. The only required correction found was a stale Mistral FIM citation URL, which was patched in the final report.

routes_used: ketch scrape for official docs, marketplace listings, and GitHub repository pages
ketch search for current Mistral FIM documentation discovery
curl fallback for Anthropic docs extraction spot check

json_validity.method: jq empty and Python json.loads after patches

citation_integrity.method: Python traversal of all citation source_refs against source_list

notes: Report covers VS Code options, Obsidian options, official subscription constraints, open-source/self-hosted paths, and shared backend implementation paths.
The report separates true inline Tab autocomplete from chat/sidebar/agent/inline-edit tools.
The report includes decision paths for no API spend, API spend, local-only/privacy-first, and highest-quality autocomplete.
Updated verification_status because T055 and T060 have now run.

No contradictory recommendation was found: Kilo ChatGPT OAuth is treated as agent-workflow support, not autocomplete billing evidence; Codex Autocomplete is treated as experimental/non-official rather than default.

runtime_testing.notes: No VS Code extension or Obsidian plugin was installed or exercised; verification is source-based.

independent_web_checks.topic: OpenAI Codex subscription access and official IDE mode
findings: Codex auth docs support both ChatGPT sign-in for subscription access and API-key sign-in for usage-based access.
Codex pricing docs list Codex in the web, CLI, and IDE extension for eligible plans and API-key usage charged at API rates.

Codex IDE feature docs describe chat, edit, approval modes, cloud delegation, web search, and image generation; a grep for autocomplete/ghost/tab-style completion did not find inline Tab autocomplete evidence.

independent_web_checks.topic: Claude subscription/API boundary and official VS Code mode
findings: Claude Help Center says Claude paid plans and Claude Console/API are separate and paid subscriptions do not include API/Console access.
Claude Help Center says Pro/Max include Claude Code in terminal with one unified subscription and separate API-credit behavior is distinct.

Claude Code VS Code docs describe a native graphical interface for plans, diffs, @-mentions, prompt boxes, permissions, sessions, and terminal access; checked text does not document live inline ghost-text Tab autocomplete.
Claude Agent SDK credit is described as starting June 15, 2026 and applying to Agent SDK/claude -p/third-party Agent SDK apps, not as an existing live autocomplete path on 2026-05-28.
independent_web_checks.retrieval_caveat: ketch scrape of https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/overview returned only 'Loading...'; this was treated as needs-review and not relied on for the core API-separate conclusion because the Help Center article directly supports that conclusion.

independent_web_checks.topic: Kilo Code and non-official Codex Autocomplete subscription/editor-mode claims
findings: Kilo autocomplete docs say FIM autocomplete is routed through Kilo Gateway, default Codestral is billed through a Kilo account or free with Mistral BYOK, and suggestions appear as ghost text accepted with Tab.

Kilo ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider docs say OAuth can use a ChatGPT subscription for core agentic workflows, but the checked autocomplete docs separately specify Kilo Gateway/BYOK for autocomplete; the report correctly avoids claiming ChatGPT subscription pays for Kilo autocomplete.

Codex Autocomplete marketplace/GitHub pages claim VS Code inline ghost-text completions with Tab and Codex OAuth using existing Codex plan limits/credits, while warning it is not official OpenAI and currently macOS-only.

Codex Autocomplete configuration docs expose a default ChatGPT Codex backend endpoint for OAuth mode and the OpenAI Responses API for API-key mode; the report correctly treats this as experimental/high-review, not default.
Continue docs support inline autocomplete, FIM-model guidance, local Ollama and Mistral/Codestral setup, and warnings that chat/thinking models can be unsuitable for autocomplete latency/format.
Windsurf docs support inline/multiline suggestions accepted with Tab.
Supermaven Marketplace supports VS Code code completion with a Supermaven account/product, not ChatGPT/Claude subscription reuse for autocomplete.
Tabby docs support completion/chat/embedding model roles and FIM-oriented completion models with local or remote HTTP connectors.
llama.vscode Marketplace supports local auto-suggest, Tab accept, context configuration, and llama.cpp/FIM model presets.

independent_web_checks.topic: Obsidian true-inline and near-scope plugin mode claims
findings: AI Autocomplete, Inscribe, Copilot auto completion, and Companion GitHub pages each support Obsidian inline/ghost-text autocomplete with Tab or equivalent accept behavior and API/local-provider setup rather than ChatGPT/Claude UI subscription reuse.

Claudian GitHub supports Obsidian chat/sidebar, inline edit, slash commands, Plan Mode, Claude Code/Codex/Opencode provider workflows, and vault read/write/search/bash; it does not present itself as continuous live ghost-text autocomplete, matching the report's near-scope classification.
The final report's original Mistral source URL now resolves/scrapes as a Vibe Code overview, not the quoted FIM evidence.

Mistral's current FIM endpoint docs directly show /v1/fim/completions and SDK examples with prompt/suffix; the Codestral model page says Codestral specializes in low-latency high-frequency FIM and code generation.
The final report was patched to cite https://docs.mistral.ai/api/endpoint/fim for the existing Mistral FIM source_ref and all six Mistral FIM citation objects.
OpenAI terms prohibit sharing account credentials, automatically/programmatically extracting data or output, and circumventing rate limits/restrictions.
Anthropic consumer terms prohibit sharing credentials/API keys, scraping/harvesting, and automated or non-human access except via API key or explicit permission.
This supports the report's recommendation not to build around browser-cookie/session scraping.

corrections_applied.description: Changed verification_status from not independently verified to independently_verified_with_minor_source_correction and added concise notes about T055/T060, lack of runtime tests, and the Mistral citation patch.

Replaced six Mistral coding/FIM citation labels/URLs and the T040:src-mistral-coding-fim source_list entry to use https://docs.mistral.ai/api/endpoint/fim, because the old code_generation URL no longer contained the quoted FIM evidence.

residual_uncertainty: No extension or plugin was installed or runtime-tested; latency, auth flows, data handling, and Tab-key conflicts need hands-on evaluation before operational adoption.
Non-official Codex Autocomplete needs policy/security review and disposable-profile runtime testing before use, despite matching the subscription-reuse autocomplete shape on paper.
Marketplace install counts, pricing, model names, and plan limits can change quickly; rerun narrow checks before purchasing or deploying.

Rendered/sessionful pages were not accessed. Anthropic API overview extraction returned an app-shell/Loading result via ketch, so that specific docs page remains needs-review, though redundant official Help Center/API billing sources support the report's key Claude API-separation claim.
